properties.name: Takijiri-oji
properties.description: One of the Five Great Oji. Gate to the sacred mountains.

properties.name: Oyunohara
properties.description: Original site of Kumano Hongu Taisha. The massive torii gate (33.9m tall, 42m wide) is the largest in the world.

properties.name: Yunomine Onsen
properties.description: Japan's oldest thermal spring (1,800+ years). Tsuboyu bath is the only UNESCO World Heritage bathing facility in the world.

properties.name: Yunomine-oji
properties.description: Oji shrine near the hot springs.

properties.name: Kumano Nachi Taisha
properties.description: Grand Shrine overlooking Nachi Falls. 800-year-old sacred camphor tree. Annual fire festival (July 14).

properties.name: Nachi Falls
properties.description: 133m tall — tallest single-drop waterfall in Japan. Worshipped as a deity (Hiryu Gongen).

properties.name: Kumano Hayatama Taisha
properties.description: Grand Shrine in Shingu. Sacred Nagi tree (planted 1159, 17.6m tall) is a designated Natural Monument.

properties.name: Takahara
properties.description: Mountain settlement known as 'the village in the mist'.

properties.name: Chikatsuyu
properties.description: Small village with guesthouses and minshuku. Key overnight stop.

nameLocalized.ja: 古道歩きの里ちかつゆ

properties.name: Tsugizakura-oji
properties.description: 800-year-old cedar trees tower over the shrine.

properties.name: Hosshinmon-oji
properties.description: Gate of Spiritual Awakening. One of the Five Great Oji. Entrance to the sacred precincts of Kumano Hongu Taisha.

nameLocalized.ja: ファミリーマート 那智勝浦店

properties.name: Mizunomi-oji
properties.description: Ruins. Known for its water source.

properties.name: Chuoshokudo Sanbo 中央食堂 さんぼう
Fushiogami-oji
properties.description: Prostrate Worship Shrine. First view of Kumano Hongu Taisha. Pilgrims traditionally fell to their knees here.

properties.name: Kumano Hongu Taisha
properties.description: The primary Grand Shrine of Kumano. Symbol: Yatagarasu (three-legged crow).
